danger_signals.visual_form: Large distinctive enemy sprites (Kraid, Ridley, Mother Brain)
danger_signals.meaning: Major obstacle requiring defeat to progress

danger_signals.visual_form: EN meter in upper-left corner decreasing
danger_signals.meaning: Damage taken; critical state when low

danger_signals.visual_form: Solid walls, sealed doors, vertical shafts without platforms
danger_signals.meaning: Progression blocked; requires specific upgrade to enter

danger_signals.visual_form: Breakable blocks and spheres in environment
danger_signals.meaning: Hazard or obstacle; bombs required to clear

safety_signals.visual_form: Item sprites dropped by defeated enemies or in isolated rooms
safety_signals.meaning: Capability enhancement; enables new movement/combat abilities

safety_signals.visual_form: Small sprite items spawning when enemies are killed
safety_signals.meaning: Health restoration; sustains player through combat
Safe passage between areas; exploration progress
Safe standing area; prevents fall damage

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Various alien creature types: flying parasites, crawlers, armored foes distributed across Zebes regions
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fortress structures on Zebes; Kraid and Ridley as regional commanders; Mother Brain as command nexus in Tourian

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Not represented as enemies; only mentioned in narrative context
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Research vessel mentioned in opening; no visual in-game representation

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Dangerous floating parasitic creatures; primary threat in final area (Tourian)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Concentrated in Tourian facility; represent biological weapon threat central to plot

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy type distribution varies by region (Brinstar, Norfair); zone-specific architecture and enemy placement
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Regional enemies patrol/spawn in designed patterns; Kraid and Ridley occupy separate strongholds

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss statue becomes shootable; new areas unlock; enemy composition changes as areas become accessible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Passage to deeper regions opens; progression toward Mother Brain becomes possible after both Kraid and Ridley fall

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Samus sprite changes to reflect new armor/ability (Ice Beam visual, Morph Ball form, Screw Attack animation)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: New movement options (climbing, morphing), new weapon effects, previously blocked areas become traversable

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tourian environment with concentrated Metroid presence; Mother Brain sprite in throne room; timed explosion sequence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Escape sequence triggered after Mother Brain defeat; lair self-destructs visually

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: EN meter critically low; character may have visual damage indicator (palette/sprite variation possible but not documented)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: One hit from most enemies ends run; invulnerability window after damage becomes tactically crucial

environmental_storytelling.example: Brinstar features ice-themed caverns; Norfair displays heat/lava-themed design; Tourian shows technological/biomechanical aesthetic. Each region's visual design reinforces Space Pirate functional specialization

Impassable walls, sealed doors, and vertical shafts without platforms visually communicate progression gates. Ice Beam required to freeze platforms, bombs required to break walls; visual structure tells player what abilities are needed
Kraid's hideout and Ridley's domain have visually distinct room layouts and enemy compositions, establishing territorial control. Larger rooms and boss arenas signal importance
Power-ups placed in dedicated rooms or after significant challenges, establishing them as major milestone acquisitions. Visual prominence of item sprites signals value
Destructible terrain and enemy placement patterns suggest prior conflict and dynamic occupation. Platform availability tells spatial stories about how areas were constructed/used

environmental_storytelling.technique: Palette and sprite variation by region
environmental_storytelling.example: Each major area (Brinstar, Norfair, Kraid's lair, Ridley's lair, Tourian) uses distinct color palettes and tileset graphics, creating visual regionalism that communicates zone identity and progression depth

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Sprite visually changes (e.g., Ice Beam palette shift, Morph Ball form); movement capabilities expand (can climb, morph, execute Screw Attack); visual feedback confirms new state
Temporary invincibility frames allow passing through enemies/projectiles; visual persistence of sprite indicates active invulnerability window
Consistent movement patterns on screen; spawn/despawn based on screen position; attack player on proximity; drop energy/ammo on defeat based on frame RNG
Occupies dedicated boss arena; blocks progression to lower Brinstar; defeat triggers statue unlocking passage
Occupies dedicated boss arena in Norfair; blocks progression to Lower Norfair; defeat triggers statue unlocking path to Tourian
Located in Tourian final chamber; commands Metroid defenses; destruction triggers facility self-destruct sequence; controls ultimate world state (game end)
Concentrated in Tourian; latch-on parasitic attack behavior; final encounter before Mother Brain; represent core threat justifying mission

notes: Metroid (1986) communicates world-state primarily through visual progression gates and sprite-based regional distinctiveness. The game lacks explicit map or HUD narrative markers; all world-state communication is implicit through accessibility (what can be reached) and visual variation (what regions look/feel different). Boss statues becoming shootable after defeat is key mechanical/visual signal for state change. No faction explicitly identified through color coding or symbols; Space Pirates represented only through enemy types and commander bosses. Environmental storytelling relies on architecture distinctiveness, placement patterns, and upgrade gating logic. The absence of a map and explicit narrative text in-game makes visual communication critical—players learn state through what they can/cannot access and how enemies/areas change after major defeats.
